items.answer: Heavy tickling that produces laughter
The pleasant smell of rain on dry earth
The rumbling sound made by a hungry stomach
Careless or illegible handwriting
A feeling of nervousness or butterflies in the stomach
The empty space in a bottle below the cork
The plastic tip at the end of a shoelace
The smooth space between your eyebrows
A small leftover amount of food or drink
A shrewd, unprincipled person, often a politician
Pleased, satisfied, and contented
To feel nauseated or to move unsteadily
The cardboard sleeve that keeps a hot cup from burning your hand
A misheard word or phrase in a song lyric
The act of stretching and yawning at the same time
Bits and pieces; miscellaneous odds and ends
To spend time aimlessly; to dawdle
To bewilder or confuse someone completely
In a direction opposite to the sun; counterclockwise
A set of 24 or 25 sheets of paper
Relating to the day before yesterday
To talk at length in a pompous, empty way
Sickness caused by excessive eating or drinking
Resembling or relating to a dragon
